justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Context coupling explicitly identified as a domain model change — BR-5 and the business_context describe this as a fundamental change to bounded-context autonomy, not a simple feature addition. (2) Data ownership question raised — escalation #3 directly asks who owns the Stock Reservation aggregate and offers three ownership options. (3) TOCTOU/simultaneous-order race condition identified — BR-3 explains the concurrent-order scenario ('two concurrent orders could each see StockLevel=1 and both succeed'), AC-3 encodes a specific two-customer race test, and edge case #2 addresses stock-receipt timing races. (4) Stock reservation vs. check distinction surfaced — the ubiquitous language explicitly defines Stock Reservation as 'does NOT yet exist in either context — must be introduced,' BR-4 separates the read-model indicator from the write-path reservation, and rejection reasons #2 forbids using the indicator for acceptance logic. (5) Escalation to MJ for architectural coupling — three formal escalations cover sync-vs-async order blocking, real-time indicator architecture, and reservation aggregate ownership, each with options and recommendations. (6) New cross-context invariant stated — BR-2 and BR-3 define the cross-context invariants (order blocking on insufficient ATP, reservation-based ATP calculation).

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough TOCTOU analysis — not just flagged but encoded into a testable acceptance criterion (AC-3) with an explicit 'exactly one succeeds' expectation
Reservation vs. check distinction is first-class: introduced in ubiquitous language, enforced in business rules, and its absence in the current model is explicitly called out
Three well-structured escalations with concrete options and recommendations, going beyond the minimum requirement of flagging MJ
Rejection reasons section acts as a negative-space definition of correctness — explicitly naming what implementations would violate the domain model
Edge cases are sophisticated (negative ATP from manual adjustments, expiry firing on already-shipped orders, CQRS read-model performance under polling load)
Low confidence score (45) is well-calibrated — the spec genuinely is ambiguous on sync-vs-async and partial fulfillment, and Bird correctly refuses to silently resolve these

BR-3 marks stock reservations as invariant:true but then acknowledges reservations don't exist yet and the mechanism is undefined — this conflates 'this constraint is necessary' with 'this constraint currently holds,' though the distinction is arguably correct for a forward-looking analysis
Business impact analysis identifies the second-order availability coupling risk: warehouse outage becomes sales outage, with circuit breaker recommendation

This is a classic cross-bounded-context coupling request that fundamentally changes the autonomy of both contexts.","bounded_context":"Cross-context integration between Warehouse Management and Sales/Order Management.
